chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْوُقُوفِ عَلَى الصَّفَا وَ الدُّعَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 141 – The pausing upon Al Safa and the supplication

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/70)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/70)

parts.text: مَوْلًى لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ

text: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) صَعِدَ الْمَرْوَةَ فَأَلْقَى نَفْسَهُ عَلَى الْحَجَرِ الَّذِي فِي أَعْلَاهَا فِي مَيْسَرَتِهَا وَ اسْتَقْبَلَ الْكَعْبَةَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Asbaat,

(It has been narrated) from a friend of Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> from the people of Al-Medina who said, ‘I saw Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> ascend Al-Marwa, so he<sup>asws</sup> attached himself<sup>asws</sup> upon the rock which was at the top part of the pathway, and faced the Kabah’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Asbat from Mawla’ of abu ‘Abd Allah, from people of al- Madinah who has said the following: “I once saw abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, climb al-Marwah and lean himself against the rock which is on top on the pathway and face al-Ka‘bah.”(Ahadith 7-8 of this Chapter are best explained in the form of fatwa).
